title: TIP Intent Contract v1
description: Per-request canonical Intent Contract emitted by the TokenPak proxy under Intent Layer Phase 0. One contract per request; mirrors the IntentContract dataclass in tokenpak/proxy/intent_contract.py and the intent_events row in ~/.tokenpak/telemetry.db. Status: draft until Intent-1 validates the shape against collected baseline telemetry. See https://docs.tokenpak.ai/protocol/intent-contract.

tip_version.description: TIP version under which this contract was emitted (per Architecture §11.7). Phase 0 emits 'TIP-1.0'.

emitted_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp at which the proxy materialized the contract (classifier output). Trace-origin field so downstream consumers can age-check contracts independently of the request_id timeline.

emitted_by.description: Identifier of the component that produced the contract — proxy build identity (e.g. 'tokenpak-proxy@1.3.6'). Trace-origin field so downstream consumers can tell which classifier version produced the contract.

contract_id.description: Opaque, sortable, ULID-shaped contract identifier mirroring the X-TokenPak-Contract-Id wire header. One-to-one with request_id in Phase 0 (diverges in Intent-2 when one contract may trigger multiple requests). Reference implementation: timestamp-prefixed hex, 29 chars. Treated as opaque by consumers.

intent_class.description: Canonical Phase-0 intent class. The 10-class enum mirrors tokenpak/proxy/intent_classifier.py:_KEYWORD_PATTERNS. 'query' is the catch-all (any sub-threshold or unclassifiable prompt lands here). The set is intentionally frozen for the duration of Phase 0 so baselines stay comparable; Intent-1 may extend it after the baseline report.

confidence.description: Classifier confidence in the assigned intent_class, in [0.0, 1.0]. The wire header X-TokenPak-Intent-Confidence formats this to two decimal places; the contract carries full precision.

subtype.description: Optional finer-grained sub-classification within intent_class. Phase 0 has no canonical subtype taxonomy; the field is reserved for Intent-1's typed-class lift. Null when absent.

risk.description: Three-bucket risk assessment for the intent. Phase 0 derivation is heuristic (intent_contract.py:derive_risk): execute/create with missing slots → high; execute/create with all slots → medium; debug/plan at low confidence → medium; everything else → low. Intent-1 lifts this into a typed RiskAssessor.

slots_present.description: Slot names the classifier identified as filled by the prompt (e.g. ['target', 'duration']). Empty array when the prompt presented no recognized slots.

slots_missing.description: Slot names required by intent_class but absent from the prompt (e.g. ['target']). Drives Intent-2's 'block on missing required slots' rule. Empty array when all required slots are filled.

intent_source.description: Identifier of the classifier implementation that produced this row. Phase 0 emits 'rule_based_v0' (intent_classifier.INTENT_SOURCE_V0). Lets Intent-1's classifier evaluation A/B-compare against rule-based output on identical telemetry.

catch_all_reason.description: Why the classifier fell back to the 'query' catch-all. Populated whenever intent_class='query' AND the fallback path was taken; null otherwise. Values mirror intent_classifier.CATCH_ALL_REASONS. Telling us *why* the classifier gave up shapes the Intent-1 design (does the gap need LLM assist, more rules, or schema work).

raw_prompt_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the raw prompt (lowercase, 64 hex chars). Dedup key only — the prompt itself is NEVER stored in the contract or in cross-request telemetry, per Architecture §7.1 prompt-locality.
